ety: Pehl. mor; Zend maoiri; S. वम्री
def: s.m. An ant; — (dialec.) rust (i.q. morća)
more: n. m. S. मयुर Pr. moro, maūro . 1. A peacock. 1. Mirag, bāṅdrā, tītar, mor, ye chāroṅ khetī ke chor . Prov. The deer, the monkey, partridge, peacock, all these four the field just rob of its rich store. 2. Chor ke ghar mor . Prov. A peacock in the thief's house. (A thing that betrays itself). ☞ The proverb is founded on the story of a peacock who swallowed a gold necklace which a thief had stolen and brought home. 3. Mor nāchtā hai, nāchtā hai, jab apne pāoṅ dekhtā hai to ro detā hai . The peacock dances, dances on, when he looks down he weeps anon (at the sight of his ugly feet). 4. Maiṅ apne murlā ko kaṛe ghaṛā dūṅ, Mujh birhan kā joṛā re . Wom. Song. With anklets I'll my peacock's legs adorn, Twin sufferers both in anguish pine love-lorn. 5. Chor jāne chor ko, aur mor jāne meṅh, Pāoṅ jāne panahī, aur nain jāne neh . Dohā. The thief thief knows, the peacock scents the coming rain, The …

n. m. 1. A twist; bend; dog's, ear; a turn (of a road); winding (of a river); doubling; turning over, back, towards, or aside. 2. Getting back; recovery (of stolen goods). 3. Tit for tat; retribution. moṛ toṛ , n. m. 1. Twists and turns; windings of a road. Is raste meṅ baṛe moṛ toṛ haiṅ . 2. Labyrinth; maze.
s. m. An ant. مور ملخ mor-malaḵẖ, adj. Innumerable (as the ants and locusts).

s. m. 1. A peacock. مور پنکهي mor-pankhī, s. f. A kind of pleasure boat, barge. مور مکٿ mor-mukuṭ, A crown or crest like that of the peacock. 2. Twist, turn. See موڙ moṛ. ¶ maur ( मौलि ) s. m. 1. The blossom of a tree, especially of the mango. 2. See موڙ mauṛ. ¶ mūr, s. m. See مول mūl.
pron. My, mine. ¶ maur, s. m. The shoulder.
subs: adj. Thick, or innumerable, as ants or locusts.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

ety: Prk. मउरो, or मोरो; S. मयूरः
def: s.m. A peacock
subs: or mor-paksh, or mor-paṅkh, s.m. Feather, or wing, of a peacock
s.f. A pleasure-boat, a barge (with a peacock for figure-head)
(S. mayūra+tutthakam), and mor-t̤ūt̤a, s.m. Blue vitriol, sulphate of copper
s.f. A peacock's gait or walk; — walking on the hands, with the feet in the air
or mor-mukaṭ, s.m. A peacock's crest; — a crown or crest like that of a peacock.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = موڙ moṛ, q.v. s.m. An ant; — (dialec.) rust (i.q. morća)
subs: adj. Thick, or innumerable, as ants or locusts.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

def: pron. (dialec.) = morā = merā, 'my,' qq.v. pron. (dialec.; f. -ī), My, mine (i.q. merā, q.v.)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑁄𑀭 (mora), from Sanskrit मयूर (mayū́ra).

ety: fr. moṛnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Turn, bend, winding (as of a road, or a river, &c.; syn. bāṅk, pher); twist; coil; convolution; writhe; twist, sprain (syn. bal, aiṅṭhan); — a turning away, or aside; turning back, or over, or down (as the leaf of a book, &c.), doubling down; a crease; a pucker; a dog's-ear; — (dialec.) driving back (of an army), repulse, defeat, rout; — a taking back, changing; getting or receiving back; return; recovery (of lost property, or stolen goods, &c.); — return, rebound, requital, retribution; — (dialec.) germination; a sprout, shoot (of corn, &c.; i.q. moṛkā); the excrescences of the hemorrhoidal veins in the disease piles; external piles (also moṛ-kā āzār; syn. moṛkā)
more: s. m. A turn, twist, convolution, writhe, sprain: fistula ( Harris ). 2. mūṛ ( मुंड ) s. m. The head. 3. mauṛ ( मौलि ) s. m. A kind of high crowned hat, worn by the bridegroom at the time of marriage.
subs: s.m. Twists and turns; windings (of a road, &c.); — a labyrinth, maze; — chopping and changing
v.n. To be turned, or twisted, or bent, &c.; to be turned back; (dialec.) to suffer defeat or rout; to be broken, or driven back in rout.

ety: Prk. मउरओ or मोरओ; S. मयूरकः
def: s.m. (dialec.) A peacock (i.q. mor).
s.m. (dialec.) = moṛ, q.v.; — 2˚ perf. part. of moṛnā, q.v. s.m. An ant; — (dialec.) rust (i.q. morća)
subs: adj. Thick, or innumerable, as ants or locusts.

ety: Prk. मोंडअं; S. मुण्डकं
def: s.m. A shaveling; a contemptuous term for a Bairāgī, &c. (i.q. munḍā, q.v.); — (dialec.) a boy.

ety: mo, q.v.+rā, gen. aff., q.v.
def: pron. (dialec.; f. -ī), My, mine (i.q. merā, q.v.)
adj. (dakh.) See مورکهـ mūrakh.
s.m. See muram, or murum.
(Braj) s.m. pl. (of mor, q.v.), Peacocks; — ants.

ety: mor, q.v.+ća, dim. aff. = ka = S. क
def: s.m. A little ant, an emmet; — (met.) a poor weak creature, or thing; — rust; — a line of intrenchment; a fortification; — a battery
subs: (or bāṅdhnā), To make (or to form) intrenchments, to intrench
adv. On the intrenchments, or fortification, or parapet; facing an enemy
To clip short the beard, &c. (so that it resembles an ant's legs); — to pluck out the beard by the root
To carry an intrenchment, to take a battery
(-kā), To take an intrenchment, or a battery; — to oppose; to make head (against)
(-kā), 1˚ = morća jītnā, q.v.; — 2˚, to oppose, to wrangle, to bandy words (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مورْچَه (mōrča).

def: s.m. corr. of مورچه morća, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. mor, 'a peacock.' s.m. An ant; — (dialec.) rust (i.q. morća)
more: s. f. Lines of intrenchment, approaches, trenches (in a fort).
subs: adj. Thick, or innumerable, as ants or locusts.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = مورچهل morćhal, q.v. s.m. A whisk, or fan, of peacock's feathers (for driving away flies, &c.); a whisk, or fan (in general).

ety: cf. morća
def: s.f. Line of intrenchment (for besieging a fortified place); approaches; — a battery (i.q. morća); — trench (in a fort).

ety: also murćaṅg, corr. of mućaṅg, q.v.
def: s.m. A jew's-harp.
more: s. f. A jews-harp. See مچنگ muchang.

def: n. f. Cor. of morchāl . An intrenchment; the embrasures (of a fort). morchā-bandī k., yā bāṅdhnā , v. a. To make an intrenchment; to intrench. morche par . Facing an enemy. morchā jītnā , v. a. To carry an entrenchment. morchā lenā , v. a. To oppose; make head against. morchā mārnā , v. a. 1. To attack the enemy with success. 2. To wrangle; bandy words with.
more: n. m. Rust ( zaṅg ). Talvār meṅ morchā lag gayā .

ety: mor+ćhāl, qq.v.; — or ˚ćhal = S. शल्यं
def: s.m. A whisk, or fan, of peacock's feathers (for driving away flies, &c.); a whisk, or fan (in general).
more: s. m. A fan for driving away flies (especially, of peacockʼs feathers), a brush.

def: s.m. = moṅḍhā, q.v. s.m. A kind of seat, or stool (made of reeds, or cane, and cord); a footstool; — the shoulder (i.q. muḍḍhā); a hump; a lump; — a gusset on the shoulder of a coat; — mouth (of a bag); — a sprout or shoot (of sugar-cane).
more: s. m. 1. A stool. 2. The shoulder. See مونڐها monḍhā.

ety: for mohrī = muhrī = Prk. मुहडिआ = S. मुख+र+इका
def: s.f. A subterranean passage for water, a pipe, a conduit; a drain, gutter, sewer, a sink; — a passage, canal (of the body, &c.); an orifice, opening (e.g. tang morī-kā paijāma)
subs: v.n. (colloq.) The bowels to be loose, or, to have loose bowels (syn. peṭ ćalnā)
s.m. A child who dies soon after birth (used by women).

ety: fem. of mor, or morā
def: s.f. A peahen (i.q. mornī, q.v.). s.f. A peahen; — a peg; — pendant (of an ear-ring, or nose-ring).
more: n. f. H. muṅh mouth. 1. A drain; a sink. 2. An orifice; opening. Taṅg morī kā paijāmā . morī par jānā , Wom. v. n. To make water; to piss. morī chhūṭnā , v. n. To be loosened (the bowels) ( peṭ chalnā ). morī kā kīṛā , A child who dies soon after birth.
s. f. A pipe or subterraneous passage (for water).

def: s.f. = mornī (the com. form), q.v. s.f. A peahen; — a peg; — pendant (of an ear-ring, or nose-ring).

ety: Prk. मोडकओ; S. मोटक+कः, rt. मुट्
def: s.m. (dialec.) A sprout, or shoot (of corn, &c.); — external piles (i.q. moṛ, and moṛ-kā āzār).
v.t. (dialec.) = موڙنا moṛnā, q.v.
more: v. a. 1. To twist; bend; turn; double. 2. To plait; gather. Gokhrū moṛnā . 3. To screw on. 4. To misconstrue; pervert; distort. Arth moṛnā . 5. To take back. muṛī ḍanḍī . ( Mech .) A bent lever.

ety: moṛ˚ = Prk. मोड(इ, or मोडे(इ); S. मोट(ति), or मोटय(ति), rt. मुट्
def: v.t. To turn, bend, twist; to sprain; to screw, or screw on; to turn or double down; to plait, gather; to turn away, or aside; to turn back; (dialec.) to drive back (an army), to repulse, defeat, put to rout; — to give back, to return; — to take back; — to twist, torture, or pervert (one's meaning, or words, e.g. arth moṛnā)
more: v. a. To twist, to turn, to bend, to screw. موڙدينا moṛ denā (dakh.) To defeat, rout, break (?). موڙ کهانا moṛ khānā (dakh. i. q. فرارهونا) To be put to flight, to flee (?).

ety: mor, q.v.+nī = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A peahen; — a peg; — pendant (of an ear-ring, or nose-ring).
more: n. f. 1. A peahen. 2. A peg. 3. A pendant of an earring or nose-ring.
s. f. 1. ( मयूरी ) A peahen. 2. (dakh.) An ornament worn on the ear or nose. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: pron. = morā = merā, qq.v. pron. (dialec.; f. -ī), My, mine (i.q. merā, q.v.)

ety: Prk. मउरओ, or मोरओ (with w inserted); S. मयूरकः
def: s.m. (rustic) A peacock (i.q. mor).
s.m. = S موشا मोषा moshā, s.f. Stealing, pilfering, filching; theft, robbery; plundering, sacking; — swindling, defrauding; embezzling.
s.f. = S موش मोष mosh, s.m. Stealing, pilfering, filching; theft, robbery; plundering, sacking; — swindling, defrauding; embezzling.
s.m. (f. -ikā), A thief, robber; a swindler.

ety: rt. mush
def: s.m. = mosh, q.v. s.m. = S موشا मोषा moshā, s.f. Stealing, pilfering, filching; theft, robbery; plundering, sacking; — swindling, defrauding; embezzling.

ety: mos˚, prob. = S. मुष्य(ति), rt. सुष्, or मुस्य(ति), rt. मुस्
def: v.t. To twist, to wring (as the neck, &c.); to press, squeeze; to suppress (feeling, or emotion), &c. (i.q. masosnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. Wom. S. मुष् break, steal, Pālī, moso theft. 1. To twist; wring. Gardan mosnā . 2. To steal; pilfer: filch. 3. To wheedle; cajole; cozen; diddle. 4. To cheat; chouse ( buttā denā , 1). Kis ko mūsūṅ, kahāṅ se kuchh lāūṅ, Dāl, āṭā, jo tum ko pahuṅchāūṅ? Wom. Mīr Taqī. 5. To suppress one's feelings; to grieve; take to heart; give way to despair. Kyā kījye, bin bole rahā to nahīṅ jātā, Allah! kahāṅ tak koī is dil ko masose . Wom. Inshā.

ety: Prk. मुट्टी, or मोट्टी; S. मूर्त्तिः
def: s.f. (occas. m.), A bundle, parcel, package, bale, load; — gross; amount, total; — a leather bucket (for raising water from a draw-well; — syn. ćaras; purwaṭ)
more: s. f. See موٿهہ moṭh. ¶ mūṭ, s. (dakh.) Cylinder ( Harris ).
subs: To sell the whole bundle, or load, &c.; to sell in the lump, or gross; to sell wholesale.

def: adj. (dialec. or rustic) = moṭā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Fat, plump, stout, corpulent; big, bulky; great, large; gross; dense, thick; coarse; inferior; capital (as a letter); material, important; rich, wealthy; — palpable, plain (e.g. moṭī bāt)
subs: To see indistinctly, to be dim-sighted
s.m. A thick reed-pen (for writing capitals with)
A wealthy or well-to-do person; a prize, a catch; — a heavy purse.

ety: Prk. मुट्टओ, or मोट्टओ; S. मूर्त्त+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Fat, plump, stout, corpulent; big, bulky; great, large; gross; dense, thick; coarse; inferior; capital (as a letter); material, important; rich, wealthy; — palpable, plain (e.g. moṭī bāt)
more: adj. Fat, corpulent, thick, coarse, great, large, big, bulky, gross.
subs: To see indistinctly, to be dim-sighted
s.m. A thick reed-pen (for writing capitals with)
A wealthy or well-to-do person; a prize, a catch; — a heavy purse.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀫𑁄𑀝𑁆𑀝 (*moṭṭa), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀫𑁄𑀝 (*moṭa). Cognate with Punjabi موٹا (moṭā).

ety: pass. part of اعتبر 'to hold in high estimation or regard,' &c., viii of عبر 'to study; to examine; to try,' &c.
def: part. adj. Held in high estimation or regard, esteemed, honoured, revered; — respectable, creditable, reputable; worthy of confidence, trustworthy, confidential, relied on; credible; true; authentic — s.m. A respectable person; a trustworthy person (i.q. mǒʻtabar ādmī)
more: adj. عبر 1. Reliable ( etbārī 1). 2. True ( ṭhīk , 14). motabar ādmī , A trustworthy person. motabar jānnā , To adopt, admit, or receive as true or genuine. motabar khabar , Authentic news.
(part. pass. VIII. of عبر) adj. Confidential, respectable, worthy of confidence, creditable, credible, relied on, reputable, trustworthy.
subs: v.t. To regard or consider as trustworthy, or creditable, or true, or genuine; to receive or admit as true, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معتبر (mu'tabar), from Arabic مُعْتَبَر (muʕtabar).

ety: fr. mǒʻtabar
def: s.f. Respectability; trustworthiness; creditability; credit.

ety: pass. part. of اعتاد 'to become accustomed or habituated to,' viii of عاد (for عود
def: part. adj. Accustomed, wonted; customary, usual; — s.f. Custom, habit, use; — a fixed quantity or allowance (of food, &c.); quantity usual or requisite to produce an effect, due quantity; due strength; — quantity; weight, bulk; size; power, strength
more: n. f. عود A fixed allowance (خوراک 1, 3).
(part. pass. VIII. of عود) 1. adj. Accustomed, customary. 2. s. f. Custom, habit, use.

ety: pass. part. of اعتّد 'to be or become numbered or counted,' &c., viii of عدّ 'to number, count,' &c.
def: part. Numbered, counted, reckoned, computed.
more: part. pass. (VIII. of عدّ Heb. ) Numbered, computed.

ety: act. part. of اعتدل 'to be or become even, or equal, or equable, or uniform,' &c., viii of عدل 'to make equal, or like,' &c.
def: adj. Equal, equable, uniform; symmetrical, proportionate, well-proportioned; — of a middling sort (in quantity or quality); moderate, temperate; tolerable; pleasant (not hot); calm; mild
more: adj. عدل Temperate; moderate; mild. mٴotadil h. , v. a. 1. To temper; qualify; modeate; modify. 2. To abate; lessen.
(part. act. VIII. of عدل) adj. Temperate, tolerable, moderate, calm. معتدل کرنا muﻌtadil karnā, To temper, attemper, moderate, qualify.
subs: v.t. To moderate, qualify, modify; to temper, attemper; to abate, to lessen.

def: s. f. Fatness, corpulency, thickness, coarseness, bigness, bulk, bulkiness, grossness.

ety: moṭā, q.v.+pā = Prk. प्पअं = S. त्व+कं; — pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं; — āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Fatness, plumpness; corpulency; bigness, bulkiness; thickness, &c. (see moṭā; and cf. muṭāpā; muṭāʼī).

ety: rt. muṭ
def: s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.

ety: act. part. of اعتقد 'to believe, to be firmly persuaded,' &c., viii of عقد 'to conclude, settle, confirm,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Believing; firmly persuaded, confident, certain, or sure (of); follower (of a creed or sect); an adherent, a faithful friend or servant
more: n. m. A believer; follower of a creed or faith. mٴotaqid h. , v. n. To believe or confide in.
(part. act. VIII. of عقد Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Confident; s. m. A believer, a faithful friend or servant, an adherent.
subs: v.n. To believe (in), to confide (in); to be firmly persuaded (of, -kā), to be confident, or certain (of).

ety: Prk. मुट्टडो or मोट्टडो, and मुट्टडओ or मोट्टडओ; S. मूर्त्त+लः and ल+कः
def: adj. (dialec. or rustic), Fat, &c. (i.q. moṭā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Fat, plump, stout, corpulent; big, bulky; great, large; gross; dense, thick; coarse; inferior; capital (as a letter); material, important; rich, wealthy; — palpable, plain (e.g. moṭī bāt)
subs: To see indistinctly, to be dim-sighted
s.m. A thick reed-pen (for writing capitals with)
A wealthy or well-to-do person; a prize, a catch; — a heavy purse.

ety: pass. part. of اعتمد 'to lean, recline, or rest (upon
def: part. adj. Relied upon, confided in; trustworthy, confidential, faithful
more: adj. Trustworthy; reliable. motamad-ilah , A reliable person.

(part. pass. VIII. of عمد Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Trust-worthy, to be relied on, hope (person hoped in), confidential, confided in, a confidant. معتمد عليه muﻌtamad aﻌlaihi, A confidant, a person relied on.
subs: or mǒʻtamad ʻalaih(i), or mǒʻtamad ilaih(i), s.m. A person upon whom one leans or relies, an object of reliance, a stay, support, hope, one in whom confidence is placed.
wety: From Arabic مُعْتَمَد (muʕtamad).

ety: moṭā, q.v.+pā = Prk. प्पअं = S. त्व+कं; — pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं; — āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Fatness, plumpness; corpulency; bigness, bulkiness; thickness, &c. (see moṭā; and cf. muṭāpā; muṭāʼī).
more: s. m. Fatness, corpulency, thickness, coarseness, bigness, bulk, bulkiness, grossness.

ety: moṭā, q.v.+pā = Prk. प्पअं = S. त्व+कं; — pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं; — āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Fatness, plumpness; corpulency; bigness, bulkiness; thickness, &c. (see moṭā; and cf. muṭāpā; muṭāʼī).
more: s. m. Fatness, corpulency, thickness, coarseness, bigness, bulk, bulkiness, grossness.

ety: moṭā, q.v.+pā = Prk. प्पअं = S. त्व+कं; — pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं; — āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Fatness, plumpness; corpulency; bigness, bulkiness; thickness, &c. (see moṭā; and cf. muṭāpā; muṭāʼī).

ety: act. part. of اعترف, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Acknowledging, confessing, making confession (of, -kā); — one who acknowledges or confesses, confessor, declarer, affirmer, avower
more: part. act. عرف One who acknowledges. motarif h. , v. n. To acknowledge; confess.
part. act. (VIII. of عرف Heb. and Chald. ) Acknowledging; declarer, confessor, affirmer, avower.
subs: (-kā), To be an acknowledger (of), to acknowledge, confess, &c.

ety: act. part. of اعترض 'to oppose, resist, or withstand,' &c., viii of عرض 'to present or offer (itself, to
def: part. adj. & s.m. Opposing, resisting, withstanding; hindering; objecting (to); interposing; — coming or extending across; transverse; — parenthetic; — an opposer; an objector, &c.
more: n. m. عرض An objector. motariz h. , v. n. 1. To object; question ( etirāz k .). 2. To hinder; obstruct; impede; come across.
part. act. (VIII. of عرض) Opposing, hindering, interposing; objector.
subs: (-kā), To be an opposer (of), to oppose, withstand; to hinder, obstruct, impede; to object (to); to come across.

ety: act. part. of اعتصم 'to hold fast or cling (to God, or religion
def: part. adj. Holding fast, or clinging (to); relying (on); attendant (on); — preserved, defended; — abstaining, or refraining (from disobedience, &c.)
more: (part. act. VIII. of عصم) adj. 1. Preserved. 2. Relying (on), attendant (on).
subs: (from disobedience, &c., by the grace of God); a servant (of God).

ety: act. part. اعتزل 'to go apart or away (from
def: s.m. A separatist, a seceder, a dissenter.

ety: rel. n. fr. al-mǒʻtazilat; — or perhaps a P. form fr. mǒʻtazil
def: s.m. A seceder, &c. ( = mǒʻtazil); an infidel, an atheist.
s.f. = moṭ, q.v. s.f. (occas. m.), A bundle, parcel, package, bale, load; — gross; amount, total; — a leather bucket (for raising water from a draw-well; — syn. ćaras; purwaṭ)

The dawn appeared. motī-chūr , n. m. 1. ( Met .) Sparkling eyes. Āṅkheṅ kyā haiṅ? motī-chūr haiṅ! 2. A sweetmeat. motī chhednā yā bīṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To bore a pearl. 2. To deflour; ravish. ety: pass. part. of أدّي 'to bring, convey, deliver; — to pay, discharge,' &c., ii of أدي not in use

Paying, discharging; performing (what is due); — causing, leading (to), conducive (to); — s.m. A payer; a performer (of what is due); — a cause; a motive.

ety: for مؤدب, pass. part. of ii of أدب 'to be courteous,' &c.
def: adj. Well-bred, well-behaved, courteous, civil, polite, respectful, reverential; — instructed, learned.
adj. Well-mannered; respectful; civil; polite.
more: (part. pass. II. of أدب) adj. Well disciplined, civil, polite, modest.

def: (part. pass. II. of ادب) adj. Wellbred, well-behaved, polite, respectful, reverential.

ety: pass. part. of أدّب, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Well-trained, well-bred; disciplined; polite, civil, courteous; respectful; modest; — instructed, learned; — well-trained, broken (an animal)
subs: adj. Politely, courteously; respectfully; modestly; meekly.

ety: for A. مؤاحنة, inf. n. of آحن 'to treat, or regard (one
def: s.f. Enmity, hostility; dislike, hatred.
more: (inf. III. of أحن) s. f. Hatred, dislike, enmity.

ety: act. part. of أحّد and وحّد 'to make, or to call, one; to declare (God
def: part. adj. & s.m. Declaring, or professing, the unity of God; orthodox; — one who believes in the unity of God; — a unitarian.

ety: muʼaḥḥid, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Like one who believes in one God.
adj. Confirmatory; corroborative.

ety: pass. part. of عيّن 'to appoint, prescribe (for
def: part. adj. Appointed, prescribed, specified, assigned; fixed, established, determined, settled; ascertained; declared, certified; — s.m. (in Geom.), A rhombus
more: adj. عين Appointed; established; fixed. muaiyan k. , v. a. To appoint; establish. muaiyan h. , v. n. To be appointed, established. vaqt-i-muaiyan , The appointed time.

(part. pass. II. of عين Heb. ) adj. Established, fixed, ascertained, appointed, certified. معين کرنا muʼaﻌiyan karnā, To fix, establish, appoint, ascertain, constitute. (Fem. معينه). ¶ muʼīﻌn (part. act. IV. of عون Heb. ) An assistant.
subs: v.t. To appoint, &c.; — to assume, to take, to fix on (as)

ety: act. part. of أيّد 'to strengthen,' &c., ii of آد (for أيد
def: part. adj. Strengthening; aiding; rendering victorious; — confirming, corroborating; confirmatory, corroborative
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Strengthened; aided; confirmed, &c.

ety: for A. مؤاخذة muʼāḵẖaẕat, inf. n. of آخذ, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.m. Calling to account; taking satisfaction (from); retaliating; punishing, chastising; punishment; impeachment; accountability; — amends, damages; — reprehension, blame
subs: vulg. muʼāḵẖaẕe-dār, adj. Accountable (for, -kā), responsible, answerable
(-ko), To relieve (one) from responsibility; to discharge from liability
(-ko), 1˚ To redeem (in law); — 2˚ i.q. muʼāḵẖaẕe-se barī karnā
v.n. To take satisfaction (from, -se); — v.t. To call to account; to impeach; to punish; — to demand as a due, to claim.

ety: pass. & act. part. of أخّر 'to cause to go back; to put back, &c.; — to go back or backwards,' ii of أخر not in use
def: part. adj. Hinder; posterior; consequent; left behind; delayed, postponed; procrastinated; — muʼaḵẖḵẖar, s.m. Hinder, back, or latter part (of anything), or the hindermost, or last, part; — the posteriors.

ety: pass. part. of أخّذ, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Taken, seized, snatched; — captivated, fascinated; — retaliated on; called to account.
more: (part. pass. II. of اخذ Heb. ) adj. Taken, seized, retaliated upon, called to account.

def: (part. act. II. of أخر Heb. ) adj. 1. Posterior, consequent. 2. Delayed, procrastinated.
(part. pass. II. of أکد) adj. Confirmed, corroborated, efficacious.

ety: pass. part. of أکّد, a dialec. var. of وکّد 'to strengthen; confirm,' &c., ii of وکد 'to stop, stand still; — to tie, fasten'; cf. tākīd
def: part. adj. Strengthened, confirmed, corroborated; — redoubled; — efficacious; powerful, strong.
part. = معلَي muʻallā, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of علّي 'to make high, to elevate, to exalt,' ii of علا (for علو
def: part. adj. High, elevated, exalted, eminent, sublime
more: adj. علو 1. Elevated; sublime; high. 2. Exalted; eminent. muallā alqāb , Of exalted rank. urdū-i-muallā, lit . the royal camp. Good Urdu (the Queen's English).
(part. pass. II. of علو Heb. and and Aram. ) adj. Exalted, high, elevated, eminent, sublime.
subs: Of exalted rank or dignity; — urdū-ě-muʻallā, s.m. 'The royal camp or court'; the court language; good Urdū (cf. 'the Queen's English').

ety: for A. مؤلفة muʼallafat, fem. of muʼallaf
def: part. = muʼallaf, q.v.
(part. pass. II. plur. of الف) s. Compilations, writings.
s.f.(?) pl. (of muʼallaf), Compilations, compositions, writings.

ety: pass. part. of علّق 'to hang, or suspend' (a thing, to
def: part. adj. Hung (to), suspended, hung up, hanging, pendulous, dangling.
more: (part. pass. II. of علق) adj. Suspended, pendulous, hanging, hung to.

ety: act. part. of ألّف 'to unite, or put together; to compile,' &c., ii of ألِف 'to keep to, cleave to,' &c.
def: s.m. One who collects or puts together; a compiler, composer, author; editor
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Compiled, composed (see muʼallafa).

def: n. m. A compiler (of a book). mual'lifah , p. p. Compiled.
more: (part. act. II. of ألف) s. m. A compiler (of a book), an author.

ety: act. part. of علّم 'to teach,' &c.
def: s.m. A teacher, tutor, instructor, preceptor, master; the pilot, or the mate (of a ship, — in the barbarous dialect of the 'lascars' called mālim, or malim); — muʻallam (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Taught, instructed; trained, disciplined; — s.m. A learner, student, pupil
more: n. m. علم A preceptor; a teacher ( pāṭhak , 1). mual'limah , n. f. A female teacher ( ustānī ). moal'lam , n. m. A student; pupil; learner.
(part. act. II. of علم) s. m. An instructor, teacher, preceptor, a pilot. ¶ muʼaﻌllam (part. pass.) Taught, instructed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَلِّم (mu'allim), borrowed from Arabic مُعَلِّم (muʕallim), from عَلَّمَ (ʕallama).

ety: for A. معلمة muʻallimat, fem. of muʻallim
def: s.f. A female teacher, an instructress, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معلمه (mu'allima, “female teacher”), from Arabic مُعَلِّمَة (muʕallima).

ety: fr. muʻallim
def: s.f. Teachership, tutorship; — instruction; — pilotage.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُعَلِّمِی (mu'allimī).

ety: pass. part. of form ii, fr. عنبر 'ambergris'
def: part. adj. Perfumed with ambergris.
more: (part. pass. from عنبر) adj. Perfumed with ambergris.

ety: for A. معمَي muʻammā, pass part. of عمّي 'to make obscure, or covert, or enigmatical,' ii of عمِي 'to be or become obscure,' &c.
def: s.m. What is made unapparent, or obscure, or difficult, or enigmatical'; a verse of mysterious meaning; — an acrostic; — an enigma; an anagram; a riddle, rebus; an innuendo; a dark saying; — a difficult or complicated matter
more: (for معميٰ part. pass. II. of عمي) s. m. An enigma, an acrostick, a verse of mysterious meaning, riddle, rebus.
subs: or muʻammā kholnā, To solve a riddle, or an enigma, &c.

def: s.m. = معما muʻammā, q.v. s.m. What is made unapparent, or obscure, or difficult, or enigmatical'; a verse of mysterious meaning; — an acrostic; — an enigma; an anagram; a riddle, rebus; an innuendo; a dark saying; — a difficult or complicated matter
subs: or muʻammā kholnā, To solve a riddle, or an enigma, &c.

def: s.m. corr. of muʻammā, q.v. s.m. What is made unapparent, or obscure, or difficult, or enigmatical'; a verse of mysterious meaning; — an acrostic; — an enigma; an anagram; a riddle, rebus; an innuendo; a dark saying; — a difficult or complicated matter
more: n. m. 1. What is made obscure or difficult to be explained. 2. A riddle; rebus; an enigma; anagram; acrostic. 3. A difficult or complicated matter. 4. An innuendo. Is muamme ko āp nahīṅ samjhe. muamma hal k., yā kholnā , To solve an enigma.
subs: or muʻammā kholnā, To solve a riddle, or an enigma, &c.

ety: pass. part. of عمر 'to prolong life; to preserve alive,' ii of عمر 'to live long'
def: part. adj. Aged; blest with long life.
more: (part. pass. II. of عمر) adj. Aged, blessed with long life.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُعَمِّر (muʕammir).

def: s.m. pl. (of muʻammā, q.v.), Enigmas, dark sayings, &c.
more: s. plur. (dakh.) of معما muʼaﻌmmā, q. v.

ety: act. part. of عمّل 'to cause to do, or work,' &c., ii of عمل 'to do,' &c.
def: part. Causing to do, or work, &c.; causing to be done or performed.

ety: for A. موانسة, inf. n. of آنس, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Sociableness, familiarity, intimacy; society, companionship, fellowship; — tameness, state of being domesticated.
more: (inf. III. of أنس Heb. ) s. f. Companionship, fellowship, society, familiarity, intimacy.

ety: pass. part. of أنّث 'to make (a noun, &c.
def: adj. Feminine, of the feminine gender; — effeminate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُؤَنَّث (mu'annas), from Arabic مُؤَنَّث (muʔannaṯ).

ety: for A. معَري muʻarrā, pass. part. of عرّي 'to make naked, or bare, to denude,' &c., ii of عري 'to be or become naked,' &c.
def: part. adj. Denuded, divested, stripped; naked; bare; bald; — free (from); pure.
more: adj. عري 1. Naked; bare ( barahnah ). 2. Pure; simple. 3. Unstamped.
(for معريٰ part. pass. II. of عري Heb. ) adj. Bare, naked, bald.

ety: for A. مؤرخة muʼarraḵẖat, fem. of muʼarraḵẖ
def: part. adj. Dated, bearing date, under date; written; — chronicled.

ety: act. part. of عرّف 'to make known,' &c., ii of عرف 'to know,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Making known; describing; introducing; praising; — one who makes known; or introduces (to), &c.
more: part. act. (II. of عرف) Making known, describing, praising; one who makes known, one who introduces. ¶ mu-aﻌrraf, part. pass. Made known.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Made known; — marked; notified; — rendered definite (a noun).

ety: act. part. of أرّخ 'to date (a letter, or writing
def: s.m. A chronicler, an annalist, a historian; a chronologer; a biographer
more: n. m. Pl. muarrikhīn . ارخ he dated it. An annalist; historian.
(part. act. II. of أرخ Heb. or perhaps, from Heb. and Chald. Month, moon, new moon) s. m. An annalist, an historian, a chronologer, biographer.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Dated; — chronicled (see muʼarraḵẖa, the more com. form).

def: (obl. pl. of muʼarriḵẖ), s.m. pl. Chroniclers, writers of annals, historians.

ety: pass. part. of the quadrilit. v. عصفر 'to dye with saffron'
def: part. Dyed with saffron; — s.m. Anything died with saffron; — maʻaṣfar, s.m. Safflower, bastard saffron (i.q. ʻus̤fur).
more: (prop. muʼaﻌṣfar, from عصفر) s. m. Safflower or bastard saffron. (Carthamus tinctorius).

def: adj. اثر marked. 1. Making an impression; taking effect; efficacious; efficient; effective ( kārgar ). 2. Affecting; touching. muassar h. , v. n. To have effect ( asar k .).
more: (part. act. II. of أثر Heb. ) adj. Penetrating, making an impression, striking, effecting, efficacious; (subst.) effector.

ety: act. part. of أثّر 'to leave or make a mark, or impression, &c. (upon
def: adj. Making an impression; penetrating; taking effect; efficacious, effective, efficient, effectual; striking; applicable; — affecting, touching
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Impressed (upon); penetrated; affected (by)
(with loc.), To prove efficacious, to have effect, &c. (i.q. as̤ar karnā, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُؤَثِّر (mu'assir), from Arabic مُؤَثِّر (muʔaṯṯir).

def: adj. f. See موثر muʼas̤s̤ir.

ety: for A. موثرة muʼas̤s̤irat, fem. of muʼas̤s̤ir
def: part. adj. = muʼas̤s̤ir.

ety: pass. part. of عطّل 'to divest of; to render vacant, or unoccupied; or free from work, or unemployed,' &c., ii of عطل 'to be destitute (of
def: part. adj. Divested (of); destitute (of); vacant, void, unoccupied; uninhabited; unfrequented; left untouched; neglected; abandoned, deserted; — free from work, unemployed, out of employ; idle; — fallow; — suspended, in abeyance; — out of use, obsolete; — empty, useless, vain, of no account
more: adj. عطل Suspended; in abeyance. muٴat'tal k. , v. a. To suspend (from office).
(part. pass. II. of عطل Heb. ) adj. Vacant, unemployed, disengaged, neglected, destitute, out of use, obsolete, idle.
subs: v.t. To keep vacant, &c.; to keep unemployed; — to keep or hold in abeyance, &c.
v.t. To render vacant or unoccupied; — to put out of employ; — to suspend (from office); to abolish, to cancel, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معطل (mu'attal), from Arabic مُعَطَّل (muʕaṭṭal).

ety: fr. muʻat̤t̤al
def: s.f. The being idle, or out of employment; — suspension (from office); — neglect; — delay.

ety: pass. part. of عطّر 'to perfume' (another, or a thing
def: part. adj. Perfumed, scented; fragrant.
more: (part. pass. II. of عطر analog. to Heb. ) adj. Perfumed, fragrant.

ety: pass. part. of عوّد 'to accustom, or habituate,' &c., ii of عاد (for عود
def: part. adj. Accustomed, habituated, addicted; exercised, trained.
more: (part. pass. II. of عود Heb. ) adj. Exercised, trained, accustomed.

def: adj. A sum; total. 1. Mavāzī do āne . 2. Mavāzī pāṅch bīghe .
more: (part. act. III. of أزي) adj. Parallel, being exactly opposite to and resembling one another.

ety: for A. مؤازٍ muʼāziṉ, act. part. of آزي 'to be opposite; to face,' &c., iii of ازي 'to put, or make, a front (to
def: adj. Facing, conironting, opposite; parallel, being exactly opposite (to), and resembling (one another); corresponding; equal; nearly, about (e.g. muʼāzī do āne, 'about two annas': — muʼāzī pāṅć bīghe 'about five bīghās').

ety: pass. part. of عذّب 'to punish; to torment, torture,' ii of عذب 'to abstain or desist (from
def: part. Punished; tormented, tortured; — made to abstain or desist (from); — prevented, let, hindered.
more: (part. pass. II. of عذب) adj. Hindered: punished, tormented.

ety: pass. part. of عظّم 'to make great, to magnify, honour,' &c., ii of عظم 'to be or become great,' &c.
def: part. Magnified; great; honoured, respected, revered.
more: (part. pass. II. of عظم Heb. ) adj. Honoured, great, respected, reverend. ¶ muﻌz̤am (part. pass. IV.) s. m. The superiour, the greater.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُعَظَّم (muʕaẓẓam).

def: (for A. معظمة muʻaz̤z̤amat, fem. of muʻaz̤z̤am), part. adj. f. = muʻaz̤z̤am, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of عزّز 'to render (one
def: part. Honoured, dignified; magnified; revered, esteemed; honourable
more: adj. Honored; revered; honorable. Muazzaz khidmat yā ohdah .
(part. pass. II. of عزّ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Honoured, revered, esteemed.
subs: v.t. To hold in honour, to honour, revere, esteem.

ety: act. part. of أذّن 'to make known, to notify, to proclaim,' &c., ii of أذن 'to know, to become informed,' &c.
def: s.m. One who proclaims (by a chant) the time of prayer; one who chants the call to prayer (from a minaret or tower of a mosque), a Muezzin.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُؤَذِّن (mu'azzin), from Arabic مُؤَذِّن (muʔaḏḏin).

def: n. m. اذن he called. The crier in a mosque who calls the people to prayer.
more: (part. act. II. of أذن Heb. ) s. m. A publick crier to prayers.

ety: for A. مبادلة mubādalat, inf. n. of iii of بدل 'to change or exchange'
def: s.m. Exchange, barter; retaliation, recompense
more: n. m. Barter ( badal , 2). mubādle kā hisāb , An exchange transaction. mubādlah, k. , v. a. To make an exchange ( badalnā , 2). mubādlah h. , v. n. To be exchanged.
(inf. III. of بدل) s. m. Exchange, barter, retaliation, recompense.
subs: (-kā), To make an exchange (of a thing, with, -se, a person)

ety: for A. مبادرة, inf. n. of iii of بدر 'to hasten'
def: s.f. Making haste; vieing (with); outstripping; — haste, expedition.
more: (inf. III. of بدر) s. f. Making haste, haste.

ety: pass. part. of بدّل 'to change,' &c., ii of بدل 'to change'
def: part. Changed, altered; exchanged; substituted; — s.m. (in Gram.) The word substituted (for another), the permutative; case in apposition.
more: adj. Changed; altered. mubaddal , n. m. Case in apposition.
(part. pass. II. of بدل) adj. Changed, exchanged, altered.

def: s.m. = mū-bāf, q.v.s.v. mū.
more: (مو Hair, and باف q. v.) s. m. A slip of red cloth, a skein of thread or a fillet of brocade tied to the end of the چونٿي chonṭī or cue of hair: see موباف mū-bāf, under مو mū. A plait of hair, a fillet or riband for the hair.

ety: pass. part. of اباح abāḥa, 'to make (a thing
def: part. adj. Allowed, or allowable (to be taken, or let alone, or done, or made use of, or possessed); allowable, lawful; — s.m. Any indifferent action (whatever a person may do or let alone); — lawful pleasure
more: adj. Allowable; lawful (جايز 2, 3). Āshaqoṅ ko sab mubāh hai. mubāh rakhnā , v. n. To hold legal or lawful. mubāh k. , v. a. To legalize; make lawful.
(part. pass. IV. of بوح) s. m. Any indifferent action, lawful pleasure.
subs: v.t. To hold lawful or allowable
v.t. To make allowable, or free, or lawful; to legalize.

ety: for A. مباحشة mubāḥas̤at, fem. inf. n. of باحث bāḥas̤a, 'to search or inquire with (another, into a thing
def: s.m. Investigation; disputation, discussion, argumentation, reasoning; a dispute, contest, debate
subs: (se), To discuss (with), to reason or argue (with), to dispute (with), &c. (see baḥs̤nā).

ety: for A. مباهاة, inf. n. of باها 'to vie or compete with for superiority in beauty or goodliness,' &c., iii of بہو 'to be or become characterized by beauty or goodliness,' &c.
def: s.f. Competing (or disputing) with for superiority in beauty or goodliness, &c.; contending for glory; glorying, boasting; vainglory, pride, arrogance
more: (inf. III. of بـﮩي) s. f. Glorying, honour.

def: (part. act. II. from باه) adj. Aphrodisiaca, provocative, what excites venery.

ety: corr. of مبيہی mubaiyihī, rel. n. fr. mubaiyih, act. part. of A. بيّه, formed fr. باه bāh, 'coition,' &c.
def: adj. Aphrodisiacal, provocative, what excites venery.
s.f. pl. Aphrodisiacal medicines; things that excite venery.

ety: act. part. of iii of بہو; see mubāhāt
def: adj. Glorious, exalted; — presuming, arrogant, proud, vain.
more: (part. act. III. of بـﮩي) adj. 1. Glorious, exalted. 2. Presuming, arrogant, proud.

def: n. m. Discussion (بحث 1). mubāhisa k. , v. a. See بحثنا
more: (inf. III. of بحث Chald. ) s. m. A dispute, disputation, contest, debate, investigation, argumentation, reasoning.

ety: pass. part. of بيّن 'to explain,' &c., ii of بين 'to become separate'
def: part. Declared, explained, illustrated; made distinct or clear.

ety: pass. part. of بخّر 'to perfume,' ii of بخر 'to send up a fume'
def: part. Fumigated (with perfume), perfumed; vaporous.

ety: for A. مبالغة mubālagat, inf. n. of بالغ 'to exceed ordinary or proper bounds,' &c., iii of بلغ 'to reach, attain (to
def: s.m. Diligence, care, utmost effort or endeavour; exaggeration; amplification; intensity; excess; hyperbole; bombast; — dwelling on (a subject)
more: n. m. Exaggeration; hyperbole. mubālga k. , v. n. To exaggerate; use hyperboles.
(inf. III. of بلغ) 1. Exaggeration, hyperbole, amplification, bombast. 2. Diligence, utmost effort or endeavour. 3. Dwelling on a subject.
subs: To exaggerate; to use hyperboles; — to insist, persist.

ety: act. part. of بالغ; see mubālaga
def: part. adj. Diligent, painstaking; who does (anything) to excess; going to extremes; exaggerating.
s.m. A pudding; a sausage.

(مبارک mubārak, q. v. and P. باد bād, Be it) May it be fortunate! hail! s. f. Congratulation, wishing success. مبارک باد کـﮩنا mubārak-bād kahnā, To congratulate, hail, felicitate, compliment, welcome.

ety: pass. part. of iii of برك; fr. barakat, q.v.
def: part. adj. Blessed; happy, fortunate, auspicious; august; sacred, holy; — intj. Welcome! well! hail! all hail! blessings (on you)!; congratulations (to you)!
more: adj. برک was blessed. 1. Blessed; holy. 2. Auspicious; fortunate. Khudā hazūr ko Salāmat rakkhe, aur Begam sāhab ko Mubārak ! Double entendre. 1. God keep you safe, and blessings on your lady be! 2. May Salamat keep you, and Mubarak your lady! mubārak , intj. Hail! all hail! welcome! Īd ke pīchhe chāṅd mubārak! Prov. After Id congratulations! (Compliments of the season after the season!). mubārak bād , intj. Hail! Good fortune attend you! mubārak-bādī gānā , v. a. To sing songs of congratulation. mubārak-bād, mubārak-bādī, mubārkī , n. f. Congratulations; blessings; good wishes. mubārak bādī denā , v. a. To congratulate; give joy; to hail. mubārak salāmat , Congratulations and thanks. Mubārak salāmat ke bād nāch shurū huā .
(part. pass. III. of برک Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Blessed, august, sacred, auspicious, holy, happy, fortunate; welcome! well! hail! all hail! blessing, congratulation.
subs: intj. May he (or it) be blessed or fortunate; good fortune attend you! hail
or mubārak-bādī, s.f. Blessings, benediction; congratulation; good wishes
(or -bādī) denā or kahnā (-ko, or -se), To wish joy (to), to congratulate, felicitate, hail; to compliment; to welcome; to wish success (to)
adj. Of happy omen; prognosticating felicity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مبارک (mubārik), from Arabic مُبَارَك (mubārak).

def: s. f. Congratulation, benediction, welcome. مبارک بادي ديني mubārak-bād-ī denī, To congratulate, give joy, hail, compliment.

ety: fr. mubārak
def: s.f. Auspiciousness; happiness; good fortune; congratulation; benediction, blessing.
more: n. f. 1. Blessing; benediction. 2. Congratulation. 3. Auspiciousness.
s. f. Auspiciousness, happiness, congratulation, benediction.

ety: pass. part. of برهن 'to adduce evidence or proof'
def: part. Proved, demonstrated; demonstrative; authentic; evident, manifest.
more: (part. pass. of quadril. برهن) adj. Demonstrative, evident, manifest.

ety: act. part. of iii of برز 'to come out or forth'
def: adj. & s.m. Ready for war, warlike; — a warrior, combatant, brave soldier, hero.
more: (part. act. III. of برز) s. m. A warriour.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic مُبَارِز (mubāriz).

ety: pass. part. of برّأ 'to make (one
def: part. adj. Held or pronounced free or clear (of), free, acquitted, absolved, exempted, far removed (from); guiltless (of)
more: adj. برا kept aloof from unclean things. Guiltless (بري 3, 4).
(part. pass. II. of برأ) adj. Free, absolved, exempted, far removed from. مبرا کرنا mubarrā karnā, To acquit, to absolve, to exempt, to free, exonerate.
subs: v.t. To free, clear, acquit, absolve, exempt, exonerate.

ety: for A. مباشرة, inf. n. of iii of بشر; see bashara
def: s.f. Lying with (a woman), copulation; — superintending, managing, or conducting (an affair) oneself; commencing or undertaking (an affair).
more: n. f. بشر he enjoyed her skin. Coition (صجت 3). mubāshrat bā-mujrimānā . G. G. Incest. mubāshrat k. , v. a. To cohabit.
(inf. III. of بشر) s. f. 1. Commencement or undertaking of any affair. 2. Congredi cum fœmina, inire eam, copulation.

ety: act. part. of بشّر 'to announce an event which rejoices, ii of بشر 'to rejoice by the annunciation of an event'; both forms are prob. fr. bashara, q.v.
def: s.m. An announcer; a bearer of good news; an evangelist.
more: (part. act. II. of بشر) s. m. A bearer of glad tidings, an evangelist.

ety: pass. part. of ii of بصر; see mubaṣṣir
def: part. Made clear or manifest; clear, perspicuous; conspicuous.

ety: act. part. of بصّر 'to cause to see,' &c., ii of بصر 'to become seeing'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Causing to see or understand; — provident; penetrating; — one who can see; — one who shows, or causes to understand.
more: (part. act. II. of بصر) adj. 1. One who can see. 2. Provident, penetrating. ¶ mubaṣṣar (part. pass.) Clear, conspicuous. ¶ mubṣir (part. act. IV. of بصر) adj. 1. Seeing. 2. Clear.

ety: act. part. of بذّر 'to dissipate, squander,' ii of بذر 'to scatter'
def: adj. & s.m. Who dissipates or squanders; extravagant, wasteful, prodigal, lavish; — an extravagant man, a spendthrift, a prodigal.

ety: fr. mubaẕẕir
def: s.f. Extravagance; prodigality; luxury.

ety: pass. part. of ابدل 'to change,' &c., iv. of بدل 'to change'
def: part. Changed, altered; exchanged; substituted; — s.m. (in Gram.) The word substituted (for another), the permutative; case in apposition.

ety: act. part. of ابدع 'to create,' iv of بدع 'to originate'
def: s.m. Creator, author, originator, inventor, founder; one who first hits upon, or strikes out, or produces; — one who feigns or tells a lie.

ety: pass. part. of ابہم 'to close or lock' (a door
def: part. adj. Occult; unknown; concealed; obscure; doubtful, uncertain, vague, indistinct; unintelligible (speech); ambiguous, equivocal; — indeterminate; indefinite
subs: 'The noun of indication,' the demonstrative pronoun (so called because, in Arabic, it has no derivative, and its root is unknown).

def: adj. بـﮩم was confused. Ambiguous; indistinct ( gol , 3).
more: (part. pass. IV. of بـﮩم Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Occult, unknown, keeping concealed, ambiguous, equivocal.

ety: prob. a corr. of A. مبيع mabīʻ, pass. part. of بيع 'to sell; to buy'
def: part. Sold; bought; venal.

ety: act. part. of ابان 'to make distinct,' &c., iv of بين 'to become separate'
def: part. adj. Distinct, clear, manifest.
more: (part. act. IV. of بين Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Manifest, clear. ¶ mubaiyan (part. pass. II. of بين) adj. Explained, illustrated.

ety: act. part. of ابرّ 'to overcome, &c., iv. of برّ 'to be pious,' 'to obey,' &c.
def: adj. Provident, prudent; — strong; superior (to), who overcomes.
more: (part. act. IV. of برّ) adj. Superiour, overcoming.

ety: act. part. of ابنا 'to cause to build,' iv of بنی 'to build'
def: s.m. One who orders or causes to build or frame; framer; author
subs: s.m. Author of mischief; transgressor; aggressor.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَبْنًى (mabnan).

ety: pass. part. of ابرم 'to twist (a rope, &c.
def: part. Twisted well; made firm by twisting together; firm, strong, solid, sound; urgent; — s.m. A garment or piece of cloth of which the thread is twisted of two strands or yarns.
more: (part. pass. IV. of برم) adj. Made firm by twisting together; firm, urgent.

ety: act. part. of ابصر 'to see,' &c., iv of بصر 'to become seeing'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Seeing; clear-sighted; clear, manifest; — one who sees, or who is clear-sighted; — one who renders intelligent.

ety: pass. part. of viii of بدأ 'to begin'
def: s.f. Beginning, commencement; (in Gram.) an inchoative (correl. of ḵẖabar); principle or subject of a proposition; a substantive noun in the nominative case
more: n. f. ( Gram .) The subject of the predicate. mubtadā o khabar , n. f. The subject and predicate.

(part. pass. VIII. of بدأ) s. f. 1. Commencement, principle, or subject of a proposition. 2. Substantive noun in the nominative case. مبتدا وخبر mubtadā-o-ḵẖabar, s. f. The subject and predicate, or substantive and adjective (in syntax).
subs: s.f. The inchoative and enunciative, the subject and predicate, the substantive and adjective.

ety: act. part. of viii of بدأ 'to begin'
def: s.m. Beginner; young scholar, a disciple just commencing to learn, a tyro; — a founder.
more: n. m. A beginner; a tyro.
(part. act. VIII. of بدأ) s. m. 1. Beginning, a young scholar, a disciple just commencing to learn, a tyro. 2. A founder, beginner.

ety: pass. part. of ابطل 'to nullify,' &c., iv of بطل 'to become vain or null'
def: part. Nullified, annulled, abolished, cancelled; annihilated.

ety: pass. part. of viii of بلو 'to test or try,' &c.
def: part. Sorely tried, afflicted, distressed, distracted, fallen (into, -meṅ, evil, or calamity, or trouble), involved (in), overtaken (by); entangled; fascinated, enamoured (of)
more: adj. Involved; entangled; implicated. mubtal'ā-i-balā, yā āfat . Overtaken by calamity or misfortune. mubtalā h. , v. n. To be involved in, entangled, implicated.
(part. pass. VIII. of بلا Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Tried or fallen (in evil, calamity, or trouble), afflicted, affected, distressed, distracted, unfortunate, fascinated, enamoured, captivated.
subs: Fallen into (or involved in, or overtaken by) calamity or misfortune
wety: Ultimately from Arabic مُبْتَلًى (mubtalan).

ety: act. part. of viii of بسم 'to smile'
def: part. adj. Smiling, laughing gently.

ety: pass. part. of viii of بذل 'to give liberally or unsparingly'
def: adj. Used every day; treated as of little value; common; contemptible, base, degenerate.
more: (part. pass. VIII. of بذل) adj. Contemptible, base, degenerate.

ety: act. part. of ابطل, iv of بطل; see mubt̤al
def: part. adj. & s.m. Nullifying, annulling, abolishing; who or what invalidates; annihilating; frustrative; — annuller, abolisher, &c.; defacer, destroyer; — one who says a thing in which is no truth or reality; one who embellishes speech with lies.
more: (part. act. IV. of بطل) Abolishing, annihilating, abolisher.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.
more: s. m. A bond, a note of hand, an agreement, a written obligation.

def: s.m. A bond, note of hand, agreement, written obligation, recognizance, engagement
more: n. m. A recognizance; bond; penal recognizance; penalty bond. Pāoṅ palaṅgiyā pe dharne na dūṅgī, lūṅgī muchalkā likhāī balam, tum ho har-jāī . To my bed you shall not come, recognizances you shall give. My husband! you're a rake. muchalkah hifz-i-aman . G. G. A bond to keep the peace. muchalkah likhnā . To execute a recognizance. muchalkah lenā, yā likhānā , v. a. To take a recognizance. muchalkah-i-nek-chalanī . A bond for good behaviour. muchalka karvānā , v. a. To cause one to execute a recognizance.
s. m. A bond, a note of hand, an agreement, a written obligation.
subs: A bond to keep the peace
(-ko), To give a written agreement, or a bond (to); to execute a recognizance
or mućalka lenā, or mućalka karwānā (-se), To cause (one) to execute a bond, &c.; to take a recognizance (from)
s.m. A bond for good behaviour.

ety: caus. of mūćnā; cf. mać-ānā
def: v.t. To cause to close or shut upon; to cause to nip or pinch.

ety: mu = muṅh+ćang, qq.v.
def: s.f. A Jew's-harp.
more: s. f. Name of a musical instrument, a Jews-harp.

ety: prob. fr. S. मुच् or the pass. मुच्य(ते). Prk. मुच्च(इ); cf. S. लोचकः
def: s.m. A large lump of flesh or meat (also written moćā).

ety: Prk. मस्सू, or मसू; S. श्मश्रु
def: s.f. Moustache; — whisker; — (met.) boasting, boast, brag
subs: 'A moustache-twirling bread-breaker,' one who is proud of eating the bread of idleness
s.m. One who shaves his moustache, or whose moustache is shaved off
(or mūćhoṅ-ko) tāʼo denā, To twirl or twist the moustache; to act the fop or swell, to be proud or vain; to boast
To pass the hand over, or to stroke, the moustache; to be proud or vain, &c. (i.q. mūćhoṅ-ko tāʼo denā)
To make an offering on the appearance of the moustache (of a son, — the offering is made by the parents, among Muḥammadans)
(-kī), To cause the moustaches to be plucked out (a mode of punishment formerly practised in India)
To turn or twist the moustaches upwards; to be vain, &c. (i.q. mūćhoṅ-ko tāʼo denā).

ety: mūćh, q.v.+Prk. अइल्लो = S. क+इलः
def: adj. & s.m. Having (or one who has) large or long moustaches.
s.m. A large, or heavy, moustache; long and thick moustache
subs: s.m. pl. Long and thick monstaches; — large, bushy whiskers.

ety: mućh˚, from S. मोक्ष्,+anauṅ = Ap. Prk. अणहं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. (old H.) To be let loose, be set free, be liberated.

ety: mućhan˚, prob. for mūćhan, dialec. pl. of mūćh, q.v.+dar = S. धरः
def: s.m. (lit. 'Moustached, or whiskered'), A rat; — a keeper or trainer of monkeys, a monkey-man; — a mountebank, merry-andrew, buffoon; a fool, an idiot; — adj. Stupid, &c.
more: n. m. 1. A fool; dunce; idiot. 2. A mountebank; a merryandrew.
1. s. m. A rat. 2. ( met. ) adj. Stupid.
n. prop. Name of a Jogī.

ety: i.q. mućhayo = mućhā, part. of mućhnauṅ, q.v.
def: part. (old H.) Loosened, set free, liberated, &c.

n. f. Pl. mūchheṅ , S. श्मश्रु Pr. massū, mansū , the beard. 1. Moustaches. Mūchh maṛoṛā, roṭī toṛā . Prov. A twirling his moustache, all day, to fill his stomach's all his care. 2. ( Met .) Brag; boast. Ghar kī korī mūchheṅ hī mūchheṅ haiṅ . Prov. mūchh kā bāl , n. m. A truthful man. Muṅh par kahe so mūchh kā bāl, Pīchhe kahe so jhāṅṭ kā bāl . Prov. Your moustache speaks before one's face, What speaks behind's your pubes base. mūchh-muṅḍā , n. m. One who shaves his moustaches. Sau gunḍānā, ek muchh-munḍā . Prov. One man with lips clean-shaven will match a hundred reprobates. mūchhoṅ par yā-ko tāo denā, mūchhoṅ par hāth phernā, mūchheṅ chaṛhānā , v. n. 1. To twirl or twist one's moustaches. 2. To think no end of oneself; to brag; show off. mūchhoṅ se chiṅgāriyāṅ nikalnā , v. n. To spit fire. Tobā! kyā khūṅkhār marduā hai! mūchhoṅ se chiṅgāriyāṅ nikaltī haiṅ . Mah. Wom. mūchheṅ ukhaṛvānā , v. n. To cause to be plucked (one's moustaches). (A native punishment). mūchhoṅ kā kūṅḍā k. , Mah. An offering made by the parents on the first appearance of moustache on the lips of the son. muchhākṛā , n. m. Long moustaches. muchhail', muchchhail , One who has long moustaches.
more: s. m. Release, liberation, deliverance, absolution, salvation, beatitude, final and eternal happiness, the liberation of the soul from the body and its exemption from further transmigration.
s. f. Whiskers. موچهـ مروڙا روٿي توڙا mūchh maroṛā roṭī toṛā, One who is proud of eating the bread of idleness.

def: s. m. Stiff and bushy whiskers.

ety: mūćh, q.v.+r = ar = Prk. अड = S. र + का
def: s.f. Down on the lips, incipient moustaches or whiskers (cf. maseṅ).
more: s. f. (dakh.) Down or small hair on the lips before the beard grows: see مسين maseṅ.

ety: said to be fr. mū, 'hair'+ćīna, 'plucking'; but prob. akin to moćnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Tweezers.

ety: mūćkā˚, prob. fr. mūćkār˚ = moć, or mūć, q.v.+S. कार
def: v.t. To sprain, to twist (i.q. moćṛānā).

s.m. The plant Pterospermum suberifolium (the flowers of which are applied for a headache); — mućukund, Name of an ancient king or Muni; — name of a son of Mandhātri (ʻwho asked the gods to grant him the enjoyment of long and unbroken sleep as a reward for the assistance he had rendered them in subduing the demons; the gods complied with his request, and decreed that whoever disturbed him should be burnt to ashes by fire emanating from his body. Krishṇa, in order to destroy Kāla-yavana, enticed him into the cave where Mućukund was asleep, and the latter, being thus roused, cast upon Kāla-yavana an angry glance which reduced him to ashes'; see translation of Vishṇu-Purāṇa, p. 567); — name of a son of Yadu; of the father of Ćandra-bhāga; of a poet of Kaśmīr; of a demon
more: A plant whose flowers are applied for a headache; Pterospermum suberifolia . Wat.
subs: s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ (lit. 'the one by whom it was vouchsafed that Kāl-yavan was destroyed by Mućukund').

def: s.m. = mūṅḍ, or mūṛ, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Shaved, bald, having no hair on the head; uncovered; — destitute of horns (an ox, or a ram), hornless (i.q. muṇḍā, q.v.); — stripped of top-leaves or branches, lopped (as a tree); pointless, blunt; (fig.) dull, obtuse; — low, mean; — s.m. A shaven head, a bald pate; a skull; — the forehead; — the head; — head, headman, chief, principal (i.q. murh, q.v.); — a tree stripped of its top branches, the trunk of a lopped tree; a pollard; — a barber; — an epithet of Rāhu; — name of a Daitya
more: s. m. The head. See مونڐ mūnḍ.
subs: (˚ḍa+ak˚), s.m. Headless letters, the Nāgarī character without the top line (as written by the Kāyaths; see kaithi, and muṛiyā)
s.m. A sect of mendicants who extort alms by threatening to split (or wound) their heads, &c.; an individual of that sect; an extortioner
s.m. The action, or the quality, of a muṇḍ-cirā; extortion

s.f. A posture in which the legs are drawn up and the head is thrust either between them, or into some corner or recess, or under some covering or covert; crouching or contracted form of the body and limbs
To lie with the head between the legs, &c.; to lie round with the knees to the stomach, and the head between them, or resting on them
or muṇḍ-mālā, s.f. A necklace or string of human skulls.

def: s.f. Pleasure, delight, joy, joyousness, gladness, happiness; — intoxication; — mud, s.f. A wife.
s.f. Pleasure, delight, joy, joyousness, gladness, happiness; — intoxication; — mud, s.f. A wife.

ety: act. part. of دبّر 'to devise, plan; manage, conduct, regulate,' &c., ii of دبر 'to follow behind,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Managing, conducting; disposing in order, regulating, governing; — skilful in devising, &c.; prudent; ingenious; politic; deliberate; — one who manages, &c. (affairs of any kind), a counsellor, minister, governor, administrator, director, &c.; — mudabbar (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Ably-conducted, properly-managed (business, &c.); — s.m. A slave set at liberty after the death of his master
more: n. m. A counsellor; minister. mudabbirān-i-saltanat . Ministers of State.

part. act. (II. of دبر Heb. and Chald. ) Disposing in order, governing. adj. Prudent, ingenious, politick, deliberate. (subst.) A counsellor, director. ¶ mudbir (part. act. IV.) adj. 1. Retiring, going back. 2. Bashful, modest.

ety: fr. mudabbir
def: s.f. Management, conduct, regulation, governance, direction; — the office or position of a mudabbir.

ety: for A. مدافعة, inf. n. of دافع, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Repelling; averting, warding off; repulsion; — protracting, deferring, delaying; postponement
more: (inf. III. of دفع) s. f. Repulsion, turning away.

ety: for A. مده mudahhiṉ, act. part. of دهّی, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Blaming; affirming (one) to be unfortunate; — being the cause of misfortune (to anyone).
more: (part. act. II. of دها) adj. Affirming (him) to be unfortunate, blaming, being the cause of misfortune (to any one).

ety: act. part. of دَحْرَجَ 'to roll along or down'
def: s.m. A roller; a revolver; — mudaḥraj (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Rolled along; rolled down; — rounded, round.

ety: for A. مداخلة mudāḵẖalat, inf. n. of داخل 'to enter with (another
def: s.m. Intermeddler, intruder.

ety: for A. مداخلة mudāḵẖalat, inf. n. of داخل 'to enter with (another
def: s.m. Intermeddler, intruder.
more: n. f. See دخل (2, 4). 1. Occupancy. 2. Interference. mudākhilat bilā-marzī . G. G. Intrusion. mudākhilāt-i-bejā , G. G. Trespass. mudākhilat-i-bejā ba-khānah , n. Lurking house trespass. mudākhilat-i-sarīh . A direct act of interference. mudākhilat karne-vālā . An intruder; intermeddler.
(inf. III. of دخل Heb. Aram. ) s. f. 1. Access, admission, ingress. 2. Entering into, intermeddling, interference, intrusion.

ety: act. part. of دقّق 'to make a minute examination,' &c.; ii of دقّ 'to be or become minute or fine'
def: part. adj. Minutely examining, nicely investigating; intimately acquainted with the minutiæ of things.
more: part. act. (II. of دقّ H.C. ) Nicely investigating, minutely examining.

ety: act. part. of دلّل, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. & s.m. Practising amorous gestures or dalliance, dallying; fondling; coaxing, wheedling; — a dallier, fondler; coaxer, wheedler
more: adj. 1. Supported by reasons or proofs. 2. Well-grounded; reasonable.
(part. act. of دلّ) adj. Wheedling, fondling, dallying.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb, prob. derived from dalīl, q.v.), part. Supported by evidence, proof, or argument; well-grounded, reasonable.

ety: pass. part. of ادام 'to cause to continue,' &c., iv of دام (for دوم
def: adj. & adv. Continuous; continual, lasting; perpetual; eternal; — continually; perpetually; always; eternally; — s.f. Wine; spirits.
more: (from دوم) 1. adv. or adj. Continually, eternally, perpetual, lasting, eternal. 2. s. f. Wine, spirits.

ety: rel. n. fr. mudām
def: adj. Continual, incessant, &c. ( = mudām).

ety: pass. part. of دمّغ, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. (lit. 'overcome or subdued by the devil'), Infatuated, stupid, foolish; proud, vain; fastidious.
more: (part. pass. II. of دمغ) adj. Proud, fastidious, foolish.

def: s.m. contrac. of next, q.v.
more: s. m. (inf. III. of درأ or دري) 1. Humility, humanity, politeness, affability, courtesy. 2. Deceit, circumvention, dissimulation.

ety: for A. مداراة, inf. n. of داري 'to treat with gentleness or blandishment, to soothe, coax,' &c., iii of دري 'to deceive, circumvent,' &c.
def: s.f. Treating kindly or courteously; gentleness, lenity; soothing; civility, affability, courtesy, politeness; — humanity; — humility; — deceit, dissimulation; circumvention
more: n. f. دري driving away. Courtesy (تواضع 1). madārāt k. , v. a. To treat.
s. f. (inf. III. of درأ or دري) 1. Humility, humanity, politeness, affability, courtesy. 2. Deceit, circumvention, dissimulation.
subs: (-kī), To show civility (to), to treat with courtesy, &c.

ety: act. part. of درّس 'to cause to read or study, to teach; to lecture,' ii of درس 'to read,' &c.
def: s.m. A lecturer, teacher, tutor, schoolmaster, professor (of a college, &c.)
more: (part. act. II. of درس Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A professor, a head of a college. ¶ mudarras (part. pass.) One taught, a student.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. & s.m. Experienced; well-taught; — one taught, a student.

ety: fr. mudarris
def: s.f. Office of professor or teacher, professorship; teachership; — public teaching.
more: s. f. Office of professor, professorship, publick teaching.

def: s.m. = muṅḍāsā, q.v. s.m. 'Head-dress,' a kind of small and loose turban
subs: (with loc.), To put on a turban; (fig. & by women) to lord it; to be saucy, or insolent
(dialec. for muṇḍāsā-band), s.m. lit. 'One wearing a turban'; a man
s.m. A term of abuse; wretch, &c. (used by angry women.

ety: mudau˚ = mundāw(nā), caus. of mūṅd(nā)+al or alā = Prk. अडो or अडओ = S. अ+लः or ल+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Sealing up, shutting or closing up; fastening, covering up.

ety: mudau˚ = mundāw(nā), caus. of mūṅd(nā)+al or alā = Prk. अडो or अडओ = S. अ+लः or ल+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Sealing up, shutting or closing up; fastening, covering up.
s.m. contrac. of next, q.v.

ety: for A. مداومة, inf. n. of داوم, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.f. Continuance; perpetuity; eternity; — continual use; assiduity.
more: (inf. III. of دوم) s. f. Eternity, continuance, perpetuity; continual use.

ety: for A. مداورة mudāwarat, inf. n. of داور 'to go round about with,' &c., iii of دور 'to go, or move, or turn round,' &c.
def: s.m. Going round along with (another), going about; surrounding, encircling, encompassing; adjusting.
more: (inf. III. of دور) s. m. Going about, surrounding, encircling, compassing, adjusting (a business).

ety: for A. مداواة, inf. n. of داوي 'to treat medically,' &c., ii of دوي 'to be or become sick or diseased,' &c.
def: s.f. Medical treatment, applying medicine or a remedy, remedying, curing; administering to the sick.
more: (inf. III. of دوي Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. Curing, healing, remedying, applying medicine.

ety: pass. part. of دوّر 'to make round,' &c., ii of دار (for دور
def: part. adj. Made round, rounded; round, circular; globular, spherical.
more: adj. دور going round. Round; circular.
(part. pass. II. of دور) adj. Round, circular.

ety: for A. مداينة, inf. n. of داين 'to deal with (one
def: s.f. Dealing in credit; mutual credit, dealing as debtor and creditor on both sides.

ety: act. part. of داين; see next
def: s.m. A creditor; — a debtor.

ety: act. part. of ادبر 'to turn back,' &c., iv of دبر 'to follow behind'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Going or turning back, retiring; retrograding; bashful, modest; — one who turns his back (on); one who retires or retreats; — one whose affairs are in a bad train, or who is retrograding in the world.

ety: fr. mudbir
def: s.f. Retrograding in fortune; adversity, ill-luck, misery.
more: s. f. The going backwards in fortune, adversity, bad luck.

def: s.m.(?) A certain dry measure (used in Egypt, Syria, and other parts), about two handfuls, and weighing two rat̤ls or a rat̤l and a third.

ety: for A. مدّعَي muddaʻā, pass. part. of اِدّعي 'to assert, or allege (against
def: part. & s.m. Asserted as a claim, claimed, sued for; alleged; pretended; meant; — what is claimed, or alleged, or pretended, or meant; desire, wish; suit; meaning, object, view; scope, tenor, drift; — object of search, stolen property
more: demanding. See اراده (2, 4, 6). 1. Object; meaning. zulf kā kholnā bahāna thā, Muddaā ham se muṅh chhupānā thā . 2. Tenor; drift; spirit. 3. ( Slang ) Girl; daughter. Bārā baras kā muddā hai . 4. Property; stolen property. muddaā bihā , The thing or object sued for or claimed. muddaā pakaṛnā , v. n. To discover stolen property. muddaā hāsil h. , v. n. To gain one's object. muddaā nikalnā , v. n. To be recovered (stolen property, etc.). muddaā yeh hai . The real object is this.
(part. pass. VIII. of دعا or دعوة) s. m. Desire, wish, meaning, suit, view, object, scope. (Plur. مدعات). مدعا عليه muddaʼāﻌ aﻌlaihi, A defendant (in law), criminal.
subs: adj. & s.m. Pretended to, claimed, sued for, contested; — the thing or object claimed or sued for, &c.
One upon whom a claim is made; a defendant in a law-suit; a criminal
A co-defendant; — muddaʻā-ʻalaih karnā (-ko), To make (one) a defendant in a case, to indict, charge, arraign
(or t̤araf) se, On the part of the defendants, or for the defence

def: part. & s.m. = مدعا muddaʻā, q.v. part. & s.m. Asserted as a claim, claimed, sued for; alleged; pretended; meant; — what is claimed, or alleged, or pretended, or meant; desire, wish; suit; meaning, object, view; scope, tenor, drift; — object of search, stolen property
more: n. m. muddaiyah , n. f. دعو demanded. Complainant; prosecutor ( dāvedār ). 1. Bhāī aisan hit na, bhāī aisan muddaī nā . E. Prov. No friend like a brother, no prosecutor (foe) like a brother. 2. Dīvān jī kā qalam ravāṅ, aur muddaī fanā! Benediction. May the minister's pen e'er flow, and death remove his foe! muddaī sharīk , n. m. A co-plaintiff. muddaī muddaā-alaih , n. m. The parties in a case; the litigant parties. muddaī h. , v. n. To claim; Sue for. muddaī, yā muddaā-alaihoṅ meṅ dākhil k ., To make a party in a suit.

(part. act. VIII. of دعا or دعوة) s. m. f. 1. A plaintiff, suitor, claimant, prosecutor; accuser. 2. An enemy. ¶ muddaʼāﻌ (part. pass.) See مدعا muddaʼāﻌ. مدعي عليه mudda-āﻌ aﻌlaihi, A defendant (in law), criminal.
subs: adj. & s.m. Pretended to, claimed, sued for, contested; — the thing or object claimed or sued for, &c.
One upon whom a claim is made; a defendant in a law-suit; a criminal
A co-defendant; — muddaʻā-ʻalaih karnā (-ko), To make (one) a defendant in a case, to indict, charge, arraign
(or t̤araf) se, On the part of the defendants, or for the defence
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُدَّعِي (muddaʕī), the informal pronunciation of مُدَّعٍ (muddaʕin), active participle of اِدَّعَى (iddaʕā).

def: s.m. pl. (of muddaʻā), Desires; suits; objects, &c.

ety: for A. مدّعٍ muddaʻiṉ, act. part. of اِدعی; see muddaʻā
def: s.m. A claimant, suitor; plaintiff (in a law-suit), complainant, prosecutor, accuser; — an enemy; — (in Pers.) a pretender, pretentious man; a boaster
subs: The plaintiff and the defendant, the parties in a law-suit, the litigant parties
(-kā), To be a claimant (of), to claim, sue for.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُدَّعِي (muddaʕī), the informal pronunciation of مُدَّعٍ (muddaʕin), active participle of اِدَّعَى (iddaʕā).

ety: for A. مدعية muddaʻiyat, fem. of muddaʻī
def: s.f. A female claimant, or plaintiff, &c.

ety: muddaʻī + A. īyat
def: s.f. The making a claim or suit (in law); the state or condition or position of a claimant or plaintiff.

ety: Prk. मुद्दडो; S. मुद्रा+रः or लः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Capital; principal; original stock; stock, store.

ety: for A. مدّة, v.n. fr. مدّ 'to extend,' &c.
def: s.f. Length of time; space or interval of time; duration; usance; — a long time or season; antiquity
more: n. f. مد extending. See عرصه 1. 1. Duration; usance. 2. A long time. muddat-ul-umar . All one's life; during life. muddat kā , A. H. adj. Old; ancient. muddat guzarnā , v. n. To elapse (a long time). muddat-i-madīd . A long space of time. muddat-i-muqarrarah . A specified term or period. muddat-i-hunḍī, mitī kī hunḍī . A bill after date. muddat huī , adv. A long time ago. ek muddat , A long time; an age.

(from مدّ Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) A space or length of time, a long time, antiquity. مدت العمر muddatu-l-uﻌmr, Space of life, all life long, during life. مدت مديد muddat-i-madīd, A long space of time.
subs: Space of life; a lifetime; — all life long; during life
adj. (f. -kī), Of long standing, old, ancient

ety: Prk. मुंढा; S. मूर्धा
def: s.m. Shoulder; the withers, fore-quarters.
n. m. The withers; shoulders; fore-quarters. muḍ'ḍhī , n. f. A log of wood.
s.f. (dialec.) A log of wood.

n. m. A defendant in a case; the adverse party. muddaā-alaih-sharīk , A co-defendant. muddaā-alaih k. , v. a. 1. ( muddaā-alaih gardānnā ). To make a defendant in a case. 2. To indict; charge. muddaā-alaih kī jānib se, muddaā-alaih kī taraf, yā or se , For the defence.

s.f. = munḍer, q.v. s.m. & f. = H منڐيرا मुंडेरा muṅḍerā [Prk. मुंडइल्लओ; S. मुण्ड+इल+कः], s.m. = H منڐيري मुंडेरी muṅḍerī [Prk. मुंडइल्लिआ; S. मुण्ड+इल+इका], s.f. Coping (of a wall, &c.); a parapet-wall (syn. kaṅgurā); — ridge (of a roof)
subs: (-kī), To cope (a wall); — to make a parapet, &c.

subs: (-kī), To cope (a wall); — to make a parapet, &c.

def: s.m. A kind of pulse, Phaseolus mungo (see mūṅg); — a cover, covering, lid.

s.m. = mudgar, q.v. s.m. A hammer, a mallet; a weapon formed like a hammer; a mace; a clod-breaker, clod-crusher (see mogrā); a rammer; — a dumb-bell, or club-like piece of wood used for exercising the arms; — a species of jasmine (supposed to be the wild variety of Jasminum zambac); — the tenth astronomical yoga or division of the moon's path.

s.m. A hammer, a mallet; a weapon formed like a hammer; a mace; a clod-breaker, clod-crusher (see mogrā); a rammer; — a dumb-bell, or club-like piece of wood used for exercising the arms; — a species of jasmine (supposed to be the wild variety of Jasminum zambac); — the tenth astronomical yoga or division of the moon's path.

ety: act. part. of ادغم 'to insert, or incorporate (one letter into another
def: part. adj. Inserting, or incorporating (a letter); joining two letters (by tashdīd)
more: part. act. (IV. of دغم) Inserting (a letter), joining two letters by tashdīd. ¶ mudg̠ẖam, part. pass. Coalesced, doubled by tashdīd.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Inserted (as one letter into another); doubled (by tashdīd).

ety: contract. of mūṛh = Prk. मुंढा; S. मूर्धा
def: s.m. The head; — head-man, chief; a ringleader; — adj. Head, chief, principal.
adv. In vain, uselessly, unprofitably, to no purpose; without any purpose or object.
s.m. A cloud; — a lover; a libertine.

ety: act. part. of ادرّ 'to cause to to flow copiously,' iv of درّ 'to flow copiously, to stream'
def: adj. Causing to flow (as milk from a camel); — causing urine to flow, diuretic.
